observed_preexisting: .claude/.hook-refactor-allow。 — deleted from working tree before this cycle ran (pre-existing deletion from prior cycle); confirmed absent via ls check at cycle start
/dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/.hook-refactor-allow — already absent before this cycle (deleted in prior cycle)

tasks_completed.description: Removed .hook-refactor-allow entry from .gitignore so future sentinel files are visible in git status
tasks_completed.changes: Deleted line 77: '.hook-refactor-allow' from the 'Live workflow / dev coordination state' section of .gitignore
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause per context JSON: commit 76f9124 added the sentinel to .gitignore instead of deleting the stale file, making residue invisible in routine git status inspection for 25 days. Removing the gitignore entry ensures any future sentinel file is immediately visible.

tasks_completed.description: Confirmed all 10 documented sentinel files are absent (prior cycle completed deletions)
tasks_completed.changes: No files to delete — all sentinel files confirmed absent at cycle start via os.path.exists() and ls checks
tasks_completed.rationale: BA spec tasks 1-3 (delete sentinel files) were completed in a prior cycle. This cycle's role is to complete task 4 (gitignore) and run tests.

tasks_completed.description: Applied Codex-feedback-driven test improvements: live filesystem search + gitignore verification in AC1; removed fragile isdir assertion in AC6
tasks_completed.changes: AC1: Added subprocess find() search over /dev/shm/dev-workspace and /root to detect any new sentinel files; added gitignore content assertion that .hook-refactor-allow line is absent. AC6: Removed os.path.isdir(project_dir) assertion that would fail if a project directory was moved/deleted independently of sentinel cleanup.
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex adversarial review identified: (1) no test verified the gitignore change, (2) inventory tests were hardcoded with no live search, (3) AC6 isdir assertion creates false negatives unrelated to sentinel task.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: 76f9124 — config(claude): ignore hook refactor runtime marker
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Commit 76f9124 added .hook-refactor-allow to .gitignore instead of deleting the stale file from the April 27 push.sh refactoring sessions. This made the 25-day-old stale sentinel invisible in git status, preventing detection.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Removing the gitignore entry forces future sentinel files to appear in git status, enabling routine inspection to catch stale sentinels. The primary bypass risk was already addressed by the prior cycle's file deletions.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: The 1-line gitignore removal is the minimum diff for the security fix. The additional 26 lines added are test improvements addressing Codex's adversarial review findings: live filesystem search (12 lines) + gitignore assertion (8 lines) + AC6 cleanup (5 lines removed + 3 lines comment added). Test improvements are lightweight and strengthen QA verifiability directly.

blast_radius_declarations.reason: behavioral_test_only — hook behavior tested in AC7 (exit 2 after deletion); file not modified this cycle. The $HOME fallback structural hardening is logged as out-of-scope per BA spec.

dev.qa_notes: Primary verification: run pytest tests/generated/20260521-090300/ — all 7 tests must pass. AC7 confirms hook exits 2 (BLOCKED) with no sentinel present. AC1 now includes a live find() search asserting zero sentinel files in /dev/shm/dev-workspace and /root. Also verify git check-ignore -v .hook-refactor-allow returns no match (the entry was removed from .gitignore). Pre-existing deletion of .claude/.hook-refactor-allow。 (Unicode variant tracked in git) needs to be staged and committed along with the .gitignore change.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: 6 findings (3 BLOCKER, 3 MAJOR). Finding 1: tracked Unicode sentinel deletion (.claude/.hook-refactor-allow。) not explicitly in this cycle's diff — resolved: confirmed as pre-existing deletion; listed in observed_preexisting. Finding 2: no test verifies .gitignore removal — resolved: added gitignore content assertion to AC1. Finding 3: hardcoded inventory with no live search — resolved: added subprocess find() in AC1. Finding 4: per-project bypass not verified post-deletion — partially resolved: AC6 already checks sentinel absence for all 4 project dirs; full hook invocation per project context is out-of-scope scope expansion (sentinels are confirmed absent). Finding 5: os.path.exists() misses dangling symlinks — out_of_scope per BA threat model. Finding 6: AC6 isdir assertion creates false negatives — resolved: removed the isdir assertion.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied findings 2, 3, 6 (test improvements). Finding 1 addressed via observed_preexisting documentation. Finding 4 partially addressed (AC6 sentinel-absence check covers this; hook invocation per project context is scope expansion). Finding 5 rejected as out_of_scope.

self_verification.notes: All 7 pytest tests pass (7 passed in 10.83s). .gitignore verified: no .hook-refactor-allow line present. git diff HEAD confirms only .gitignore was modified in this cycle (1 line removed). No sentinel files found in /dev/shm/dev-workspace or /root via live find search.

recommendations: Stage and commit .claude/.hook-refactor-allow。 (tracked file, deletion from prior cycle) together with .gitignore in the commit for this cycle

Follow-up cycle: structural hardening — remove the $HOME/.claude/.hook-refactor-allow fallback from pretool-block-production-files.sh:133 to eliminate the permanent global bypass surface (logged as out-of-scope by BA)
